reasoning: PoC reproduces the mid-vote addStake inflation attack end-to-end on main (a623eab). Voter creates a proposal with the pool's snapshot-era 10 ETH voting power, partial-votes 1 ETH yes, staker calls addStake{value: 90 ETH}() which auto-extends lockedUntil past the proposal's expirationTime, voter votes again (capped by getRemainingVotingPower's live read of the now-inflated pool power) — cumulative yesVotes reaches 100 ETH, strictly greater than the 10 ETH that was in effect at proposal creation. Attempt 1 failed because vm.etch of Governance zeroed the nextProposalId state initialiser (= 1); fixed by deploying Governance directly since nothing in the exploit path requires the canonical SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE slot. Attempt 2 passes. Static EXISTS verdict confirmed.
